codeReview.summary: The follow-up resolves the prior non-blocking rigor gap without regressing VAL-TOKEN-015 or VAL-TOKEN-016. The whale-vault estimate still intentionally runs only on stake, total staked, vault capacity, and the per-wallet cap (`src/content/vault-capacity.ts:141-199`, `src/components/tokenomics/VaultCapacityModel.tsx:100-103`), but the surface now explicitly tells users that whale count and stake distribution are informational-only context while vault capacity is the calculation input (`src/content/vault-capacity.ts:102-115`, `src/components/tokenomics/VaultCapacityModel.tsx:299-355`). The follow-up also adds focused data-model and UI coverage for the new assumption-role framing (`src/content/__tests__/valuation-corridors.test.ts:175-183`, `src/components/tokenomics/__tests__/valuation-and-vault-ui.test.tsx:128-147`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: `SourceCue.note` looks like a place to carry evidence-framing details, but the current `EvidenceHook` component does not render note text. Workers can update a source note expecting visible evidence-copy changes even though the rendered hook still only shows the label/as-of metadata.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: This fix updates the whale-vault source note in `src/content/vault-capacity.ts:113-114`, but `src/components/EvidenceHook.tsx:18-23` and `:29-39` only render the source label, optional as-of text, and href state; they never render `source.note`.

summary: Pass. The follow-up directly addresses the earlier rigor finding by relabeling whale count and stake distribution as informational-only context next to the whale-vault assumptions while preserving vault capacity as the actual calculation input. I found no regressions in the valuation-corridor surface or the stake-share modeling requirements from VAL-TOKEN-015/016.
